response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates exceptional technical depth with 20 years of development experience and relevant developer tools background (test runners, CI/CD infrastructure, code generators), they lack the critical people management experience required for this role. The job explicitly requires 2 years of people management and team leadership; the resume shows zero years of direct reports, performance feedback, or formal team leadership. The candidate has strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing capabilities through workshops and refactoring dojos, but these technical teaching activities differ fundamentally from managing people, setting performance expectations, and conducting 1-on-1s. This represents a minimum requirement gap that cannot be overlooked for a full-time people-leadership role.
strengths: Exceptional technical depth: 20 years of software development across multiple platforms and domains with current staff engineer role enabling hands-on code contribution and system design validation

Developer tools and infrastructure background: Created test runners (Trial), contributed to CI/CD automation (GitLab, Docker, Artifactory), and currently building MCP code generators and agentic frameworks
Strong knowledge-sharing capabilities: Organized refactoring dojos, delivered workshops and talks, taught Selenium courses—demonstrating ability to develop others and communicate technical concepts
concerns: No people management experience: Zero direct reports, team leadership, performance reviews, or 1-on-1 feedback experience despite this being a core minimum requirement and primary job function

Limited organizational-scale leadership: No evidence of setting team priorities aligned with organizational goals, leading organizational change initiatives, or managing cross-functional team dependencies
Generalist technical background: Career spans many domains and languages without deep specialization in engineering productivity, build systems, or developer tools—the focus area for this role
